title: Password Reset Safe Baseline
purpose: Defines expected safe behaviour for password reset release tickets.

referenceArtifacts.title: Reset token expiry
referenceArtifacts.body: Password reset tokens should expire within a short, controlled time window. Excessively long expiry windows increase account takeover risk if a reset email or token is accessed by the wrong person.

referenceArtifacts.title: Single-use reset token behaviour
referenceArtifacts.body: A password reset token should be invalidated after it is used successfully, so the same token cannot be reused for another password change.

referenceArtifacts.title: Regression testing expectation
referenceArtifacts.body: Changes to account recovery should receive regression testing beyond a happy-path smoke test because the feature affects authentication-adjacent behaviour.

referenceArtifacts.title: Security-relevant release note context
referenceArtifacts.body: If a release changes account recovery behaviour, the release notes or release checklist should clearly identify any security-relevant behaviour changes.

referenceArtifacts.title: Prior reset-email report review
referenceArtifacts.body: Previous reports of unsolicited reset emails should be reviewed before shipping account recovery changes because they may indicate user trust, privacy, or abuse concerns.
